name: Sacha Nelson
education: B.A., B.S., Brown University; M.D., Ph.D., UCSD; Post-Doc, M.I.T.
bio: I love collaborating with this great group of people. Other interests include ultimate frisbee, politics, music and the future of scientific publication.

name: Erick Barrios
bio: I am studying how circadian transcription factors regulate homeostatic plasticity

name: Elly Ragone
bio: I am studying how BMAL1 regulates cortical excitability and cortical development

name: Morgan Baciu
bio: I am using CRISPRi to manipulate transcription factors in mammalian cortical neurons.

name: Ryan Kirk
education: Bachelor's degree: Oberlin and University of Delaware
bio: I am interested in new techniques for manipulating nuclear proteins in order to better understand transcriptional circuits in mammalian neurons.

name: Zhe Meng
education: Huazhong University of Science & Technology at Hankou, China, B.S., Chemical Engineering
bio: I joined the Nelson lab in 2004. I really enjoy working here at Brandeis. The people are enthusiastic about their work and a pleasure to work with.

name: Derek Wise
role: Graduate Student (jointly supervised by Steve Van Hooser)
education: B.A.: Colby College, Maine; Graduated January, 2023
bio: I am using calcium imaging and visualization of synaptic proteins to monitor homeostatic regulation of network activity in cortical slice cultures.

name: Erin Clark
education: PhD, Harvard Medical School, Boston; Bachelors, University of Texas at Austin
bio: I am using transgenic mouse lines to characterize the regulatory networks that control adult neuronal identity. Now a Principle Scientist in Pharmacology & Discovery Sciences at Sunovion.

name: David Levitan
bio: I am interested in uncovering the roles that specific cell types play in learning and memory by examining their gene expression patterns in response to varying memory related tasks. Now a postdoc at Hebrew University.

name: Chenghao Liu
role: Graduate Student (jointly supervised by Don Katz)
bio: I am studying the cellular and molecular basis of conditioned taste aversion, a form of one-trial gustatory learning. Now a postdoctoral fellow at MGH.

name: Michael Schneider
bio: Recent lab member, now graduate student in Biomedical Engineering at U. Conn.

name: Anton Shulmann
bio: I'm interested in using computational analyses to learn about brain function from genomic data. Now a Research Fellow at NIMH Intramural.

name: Vera Valakh
bio: I am studying the transcriptional regulation of homeostatic plasticity, and its role in seizure disorders. Now a Senior Scientist at Cognesy Therapeutics.
